content.full_text:
State of California 
Memorandum 
To 
:Adv 
From 
: 
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION 
Barbara A. Milman 
~b~~ FPPC Statement of Incompatible Activities 
Dote Qct. 25, 1984 
No. M-84-269 
Section V of the FPPC Statement of Incompatible Activities 
("SIA") prohibits FPPC commissioners and staff from accepting 
gifts or loans from, among others, "agency officials" and persons 
listed in Section 87200. 
The purpose of Section V of the SIA is 
to prevent FPPC staff and commissioners from accepting favors 
from persons who are subject to FPPC regulation and enforcement. 
This includes all candidates, lobbyists, Article 2 filers, and 
designated employees other than FPPC employees and commissioners, 
since determinations regarding the FPPC are made by the Attorney 
Gener 
Sections 87500(h), 91001, 82011(d). 
Agency official is defined in the Act, Section 82004, as an 
agency member or employee who participates in administrative 
actions. 
The definition in Section 82004 is designed to deal 
with lobbyists, and is not relevant to the use of the term in the 
SIA. 
Section 87200 filers include FPPC commissioners. 
In 
keeping with the apparent purpose of the SIA, Section V should 
not be interpreted literally. 
Agency official should be 
interpreted to mean a designated employee of any agency other 
than the FPPC. 
Similarly, the prohibition in Section V of the 
SlA against receiving gifts or loans from persons listed in 
Section 87200 should be interpreted to apply to Article 2 filers 
other than FPPC commissioners. 
BAM:plh

sections.conclusion: Section V of the SIA should not be interpreted literally. 'Agency official' should be interpreted to mean a designated employee of any agency other than the FPPC. The prohibition against receiving gifts or loans from persons listed in Section 87200 should be interpreted to apply to Article 2 filers other than FPPC commissioners.
sections.question_synthetic: How should Section V of the FPPC Statement of Incompatible Activities be interpreted regarding prohibitions on FPPC staff and commissioners accepting gifts or loans from 'agency officials' and Section 87200 filers?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC advises that 'agency official' in the SIA should mean designated employees of non-FPPC agencies, and Section 87200 gift prohibitions should apply to Article 2 filers excluding FPPC commissioners themselves. This non-literal interpretation aligns with the SIA's purpose of preventing conflicts of interest with regulated entities.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: No formal QUESTION or CONCLUSION headers present; document is a memorandum providing interpretive guidance. The legal analysis is embedded in narrative paragraphs. Document number appears as M-84-269; appears to be internal FPPC guidance rather than response to external requester.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: How should Section V of the FPPC Statement of Incompatible Activities be interpreted regarding prohibitions on FPPC staff and commissioners accepting gifts or loans from 'agency officials' and Section 87200 filers?

CONCLUSION: Section V of the SIA should not be interpreted literally. 'Agency official' should be interpreted to mean a designated employee of any agency other than the FPPC. The prohibition against receiving gifts or loans from persons listed in Section 87200 should be interpreted to apply to Article 2 filers other than FPPC commissioners.

Milman ~b~~ FPPC Statement of Incompatible Activities Dote Qct. 25, 1984 No. M-84-269 Section V of the FPPC Statement of Incompatible Activities ("SIA") prohibits FPPC commissioners and staff from accepting gifts or loans from, among others, "agency officials" and persons listed in Section 87200. The purpose of Section V of the SIA is to prevent FPPC staff and commissioners from accepting favors from persons who are subject to FPPC regulation and enforcement. This includes all candidates, lobbyists, Article 2 filers, and designated employees other than FPPC employees and commissioners, since determinations regarding the FPPC are made by the Attorney Gener Sections 87500(h), 91001, 82011(d). Agency official is defined in the Act, Section 82004, as an agency member or employee who participates in administrative actions. The definition in Section 82004 is designed to deal with lobbyists, and is not relevant to the use of the term in the SIA. Section 87200 filers include FPPC commissioners. In keeping with the apparent purpose of the SIA, Section V should not be interpreted literally. Agency official should be interpreted to mean a designated employee of any agency other than the FPPC. Similarly, the prohibition in Section V of the SlA against receiving gifts or loans from persons listed in Section 87200 should be interpreted to apply to Article 2 filers other than FPPC commissioners. embedding.summary: FPPC memorandum addressing interpretation of Section V of the Statement of Incompatible Activities regarding gift and loan prohibitions for FPPC staff and commissioners. The FPPC provides guidance that the restrictions should be interpreted functionally rather than literally to prevent conflicts with regulated entities.
